title: Define candidate assessment bundle for score-version comparison
description:
## Summary
Define one canonical candidate assessment bundle contract for score-version comparison.

## Canonical bundle contents
- identity: scorecard, score, baseline_version, candidate_version.
- stage_runs: evaluation IDs, dataset IDs when used, sample protocol, status, timestamps.
- metrics: AC1/accuracy/precision/recall and deltas.
- malfunction context: category shares, red flags, primary_next_action.
- decision: accept/reject/inconclusive with machine-readable reason.

## Storage contract
- Full bundle JSON persisted as attachment.
- Compact summary fields persisted on task/evaluation-facing records for fast UI queries.
- One-path persistence contract: no duplicate/fallback persistence paths.

## Acceptance criteria
- Bundle schema is deterministic and test-validated.
- Decision output is reproducible from persisted bundle + referenced evaluations.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Starting implementation slice: define canonical candidate assessment bundle JSON contract + attachment/summary persistence interface in shared CLI module, with deterministic schema tests before runner wiring.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Implemented canonical candidate assessment bundle contract in shared CLI module: create_candidate_assessment_bundle(), compact summary builder, deterministic attachment key/payload builders, and accept-gate validation requiring random_gate sample_size>=200 with completed statuses. Added unit tests covering gate enforcement, bundle normalization, compact summary projection, and deterministic attachment output. Validation: pytest -q plexus/cli/shared/candidate_assessment_bundle_test.py plexus/cli/shared/evaluation_value_function_test.py plexus/cli/shared/optimization_acceptance_policy_test.py (12 passed).
